lede: A look at June 2 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Tomasz Wróblewski and Marcel Reich-Ranicki, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2023 Odisha train collision
events.desc: A collision between two passenger trains and a parked freight train near the city of Balasor, Odisha in eastern India, results in 296 deaths and more than 1,200 people injured.

events.title: Ankara
events.desc: Following a request from Ankara, the United Nations officially changed the name of the Republic of Turkey in the organization from what was previously known as "Turkey" to "Türkiye".

events.title: Mars
events.desc: Europe launches its first voyage to another planet, Mars. The European Space Agency's Mars Express probe launches from the Baikonur space center in Kazakhstan.

events.title: Denver
events.desc: In Denver, Timothy McVeigh is convicted on 15 counts of murder and conspiracy for his role in the 1995 bombing of the Alfred P. Murrah Federal Building in Oklahoma City, in which 168 people died. He was executed four years later.

events.title: Pope John Paul II
events.desc: Pope John Paul II starts his first official visit to his native Poland, becoming the first Pope to visit a Communist country.

events.title: Soviet Union
events.desc: The USSR and Yugoslavia sign the Belgrade declaration and thus normalize relations between the two countries, discontinued since 1948.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: German paratroopers murder Greek civilians in the villages of Kondomari and Alikianos.

events.title: Anarchism
events.desc: Anarchists simultaneously set off bombs in eight separate U.S. cities.

events.title: Napoleonic Wars
events.desc: Napoleonic Wars: A Franco-Spanish fleet recaptures from the British the island of Diamond Rock, which guards the entrance to the bay leading to Fort-de-France, Martinique.

events.title: French Revolution
events.desc: French Revolution: François Hanriot, leader of the Parisian National Guard, arrests 22 Girondists selected by Jean-Paul Marat, setting the stage for the Reign of Terror.

births.name: Pope Pius X
births.line: Head of the Catholic Church from 1903 to 1914

births.name: Duchess Alexandra of Oldenburg
births.line: Grand Duchess Alexandra Petrovna of Russia

deaths.text: Raj Kapoor, Indian actor, director, and producer
Killing of Benno Ohnesorg, German student and activist

observances.text: Christian feast day: Martyrs of Lyon, including Blandina
